---
title: "Research Direction Title"
description: "A research-facing overview of one active area and the math it depends on."
page-type: research-direction
status: draft
update-status: monitor
level: research
topic: replace-me
prerequisites:
  - /topics/advanced/index.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Understand the core problem families."
  - "Recognize the main mathematical tools."
  - "Know where to look for representative papers."
applications:
  - replace-me
keywords:
  - research direction
  - replace-me
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: quarterly
date-modified: last-modified
draft: true
---

<!--
Before writing:
1. Do a live source sweep with one venue or research-group page, one survey or notes source, and one representative recent paper
2. Avoid writing a bibliography dump; organize by problem families and math tools
3. Update freshness fields after checking sources
-->

## Direction Summary

State what the area studies and why it matters now.

## Core Math

- tool 1
- tool 2
- tool 3

## Representative Problems

Describe the main question types in the area.

## Representative Venues

List the conferences, journals, or communities where this work appears.

## Starter Reading Trail

Start with one survey or notes source, then one bridge paper, then one recent paper.

## Open Questions

- open question 1
- open question 2

## What To Learn Next

Point to the next site pages or prerequisites a reader should study.

## Sources and Further Reading

- [Venue or research page](https://example.com) - `Second pass` - what ecosystem it shows
- [Survey or notes](https://example.com) - `First pass` - why it is a good entry
- [Recent paper](https://example.com) - `Paper bridge` - what active direction it shows
